concept: Low alignment (align2) for unaligned data - fallback mode

task.description: Mixed-precision GEMM with 2-element alignment. Used when matrix dimensions or base pointers are not aligned to 8 or 16 bytes. Slower due to narrower memory transactions.

analysis: No vectorization possible at all in naive version.

schedule.load_strategy: 32-bit loads (2 x fp16), 4x more transactions than align8

schedule_reasoning: ALIGNMENT 2: Each load fetches only 2 fp16 elements (4 bytes). This is the minimum alignment CUTLASS supports for fp16.
MEMORY BANDWIDTH IMPACT: To load a 128x32 tile of A, align8 needs 128*32/8 = 512 transactions. Align2 needs 128*32/2 = 2048 transactions. 4x more transactions = 4x longer load time.
WHEN REQUIRED: Matrices with dimensions not divisible by 8, or when base pointer is only 4-byte aligned. Common with dynamic shapes or after slicing operations.
SAME COMPUTE: Once data is in shared memory, tensor core compute is identical. Only the global->shared copy is slower.
PERFORMANCE GAP: Typically 20-40% slower than align8 due to memory bottleneck. The gap is larger for memory-bound problems (small K).
ALTERNATIVE: Pad matrices to align8-compatible dimensions before GEMM. Worth it if doing multiple GEMMs on same data.
optimized_kernel:
// cutlass_tensorop_s16816gemm_f16_128x128_32x3_nn_align2
using cutlass_tensorop_s16816gemm_f16_128x128_32x3_nn_align2_base =
  typename cutlass::gemm::kernel::DefaultGemmUniversal<
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, cutlass::ComplexTransform::kNone, 2,
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, cutlass::ComplexTransform::kNone, 2,
    float, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, float,
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp, cutlass::arch::Sm80,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 128, 32>,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<64, 64, 32>,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 16>,
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombination<float, 4, float, float>,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<8>, 3,
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAdd
>::GemmKernel;

concept: Medium alignment (align4) - compatible with most allocations

task.description: Mixed-precision GEMM with 4-element alignment. Middle ground between flexibility and performance. Works with most standard allocations.

schedule.load_strategy: 64-bit loads (4 x fp16), 2x more transactions than align8

schedule_reasoning: ALIGNMENT 4: Each load fetches 4 fp16 elements (8 bytes) = one 64-bit transaction.
MEMORY EFFICIENCY: 2x fewer transactions than align2, but still 2x more than optimal align8.
COMMON USE CASE: cudaMalloc guarantees 256-byte alignment, so align4/8 always work for base pointer. But row stride may only be align4 if K is divisible by 4 but not 8.
EPILOGUE VECTOR WIDTH: Note the epilogue uses 4-element vectors regardless of alignment, matching this config well.
PERFORMANCE: ~10-20% slower than align8, significantly better than align2.
optimized_kernel:
// cutlass_tensorop_s16816gemm_f16_128x128_32x3_nn_align4
using cutlass_tensorop_s16816gemm_f16_128x128_32x3_nn_align4_base =
  typename cutlass::gemm::kernel::DefaultGemmUniversal<
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, cutlass::ComplexTransform::kNone, 4,
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, cutlass::ComplexTransform::kNone, 4,
    float, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, float,
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp, cutlass::arch::Sm80,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 128, 32>,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<64, 64, 32>,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 16>,
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombination<float, 4, float, float>,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<8>, 3,
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAdd
>::GemmKernel;

task.description: Mixed-precision GEMM with 8-element alignment. Maximum memory bandwidth utilization through 128-bit vectorized loads. Requires dimensions divisible by 8.

analysis: Scalar loads waste memory bandwidth potential.

schedule.load_strategy: 128-bit loads (8 x fp16 = LDG.128), maximum bandwidth

schedule_reasoning: ALIGNMENT 8: Each thread loads 8 fp16 elements (16 bytes) in a single 128-bit transaction. This is the maximum load width on Ampere.
MAXIMUM BANDWIDTH: 128-bit loads achieve full memory bandwidth. On A100 (2TB/s HBM), this is critical for approaching peak throughput.
CP.ASYNC.CG: The 128-bit async copy instruction (cp.async.cg.shared.global) bypasses L1 cache and loads directly to shared memory, freeing L1 for other uses.
CONSTRAINT: Requires K dimension divisible by 8, and row stride (lda, ldb) divisible by 8. For standard power-of-2 dimensions, this is automatic.
ALWAYS PREFER: When data layout permits, always use align8. The performance difference vs align4 is 10-20%, vs align2 is 30-50%.
PADDING STRATEGY: If dimensions are not align8-compatible, consider padding to the next multiple of 8. Memory overhead is minimal for large matrices.
optimized_kernel:
// cutlass_tensorop_s16816gemm_f16_128x128_32x3_nn_align8
using cutlass_tensorop_s16816gemm_f16_128x128_32x3_nn_align8_base =
  typename cutlass::gemm::kernel::DefaultGemmUniversal<
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, cutlass::ComplexTransform::kNone, 8,
    cutlass::half_t, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, cutlass::ComplexTransform::kNone, 8,
    float, cutlass::layout::RowMajor, float,
    cutlass::arch::OpClassTensorOp, cutlass::arch::Sm80,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<128, 128, 32>,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<64, 64, 32>,
    cutlass::gemm::GemmShape<16, 8, 16>,
    cutlass::epilogue::thread::LinearCombination<float, 4, float, float>,
    cutlass::gemm::threadblock::GemmIdentityThreadblockSwizzle<8>, 3,
    cutlass::arch::OpMultiplyAdd
>::GemmKernel;
